title: Learn KB article front matter
description: Contract for the YAML front matter at the top of every article in apps/macos/Talkie/Resources/Learn/Content/articles/. The Markdown body that follows the front matter is free-form prose.

id.description: Stable kebab-case identifier. Used as the article's slug and as the value for related[] entries in other articles. Must match the filename: articles/<id>.md.

title.description: Short article title. Rendered as the h1 above the body.

summary.description: One-sentence lead. Rendered as the article's snippet in the KB sidebar and as the search-result preview.

category.description: Top-level grouping in the KB sidebar. Stable set — add a new category only after talking with the maintainers.

tags.description: Free-form retrieval hints. Lowercase, hyphenated. Used by the agent to retrieve relevant articles for an Ask Talkie query.

updated.description: Date of the last meaningful edit. ISO 8601 date only.

surfaces.description: Native-bridge links rendered as 'Open in Talkie' pills. The URL is a talkie:// scheme path the shell maps onto a NavigationSection / SettingsSection (see README → Bridge URLs).

label.description: Short button label (sentence case, max ~3 words).

url.description: talkie:// bridge URL. Existing schemes: home, agent, library, compose, speak, settings, settings/agent. Proposed (see README): open/<section>, settings/<section>, tray, tray/viewer, workflows/run.

shortcuts.description: Keyboard shortcuts relevant to the article. Rendered as kbd badges in a side rail. Mirror the format that HotkeyConfig.displayString produces.

default.description: Optional. False if the chord is configurable and the user may have rebound it. Authors should leave true for hard-coded chords and false for HotkeyAction bindings.

related.description: Sibling article ids. Rendered as 'Related' cards at the bottom of the article. Bidirectional links are not required — the KB collapses duplicates.

agent_facts.description: High-signal sentences the Ask Talkie box can quote verbatim. Keep one fact per line, ≤180 chars. The agent treats these as ground truth and is permitted to use them without paraphrase.
